description_long: This object consists of three metal open-ended wrenches, each with a distinct size and shape, arranged upright and closely grouped together. The wrenches are made of dark, aged steel, exhibiting a patina of rust and wear, especially along the jaws and handles. The open ends of the wrenches are U-shaped, designed to fit over hexagonal nuts and bolts, with each wrench featuring two different jaw sizes at either end. The surfaces are rough and matte, with visible pitting and discoloration from oxidation, giving them a well-used, industrial appearance. The edges and corners are slightly rounded from prolonged use, and the overall form is utilitarian and robust. These wrenches are typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance areas.
description: A set of three aged, open-ended steel wrenches with visible rust and wear, featuring U-shaped jaws and a rough, matte finish. The wrenches are grouped together, showing signs of heavy use and industrial character.
description_view_0: Three wrenches are seen from above, showing their open jaws and the varying widths of each tool, with rust and wear clearly visible on the surfaces.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the thinness of the wrenches and their flat, elongated shapes, with the jaws and handles aligned vertically.
description_view_2: The wrenches are viewed from the opposite top angle, highlighting the open ends and the differences in jaw sizes, as well as the surface texture.
description_view_3: Another side profile, similar to view 1, but from the opposite direction, again emphasizing the flatness and alignment of the wrenches.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Significant rust and wear marks are present across all surfaces, especially on the jaws and handles, with pitting and discoloration throughout.
